title: Mundaka Upanishad: Two Birds on One Tree
description: Two birds sit on one tree: one eats the fruit, the other watches — the self and the Self.
introduction: Two birds sit on one tree: one eats the fruit, the other watches — the self and the Self.

translation.original_text: dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā samānaṃ vṛkṣaṃ pariṣasvajāte | tayor anyaḥ pippalaṃ svādv atty anaśnann anyo abhicākaśīti
translation.text: Two birds, companions and friends, cling to the same tree. One eats the sweet fruit; the other looks on without eating.
